latest.content:
13 Jul 2026 13:55
RNS Number : 1346M
Watkin Jones plc
13 July 2026
13 July 2026
Watkin Jones plc
('Watkin Jones' or the 'Company')
Issue of Sharesave options and PDMR shareholdings
Watkin Jones announces that on 10 July 2026, the Company granted options ("Options") over
2,217,345
ordinary shares pursuant to the Watkin Jones plc Sharesave Scheme.
The Options have a contract start date of 1 August 2026 and normally vest and become exercisable on completion of the three savings period. The exercise price, which was set in accordance with HM Revenue & Customs rules, is 16.80 pence per ordinary share.
Within the Options described above, Options over 65,000 ordinary shares were granted to Alex Pease, Chief Executive Officer. The Notification of Dealing Form provided in accordance with the requirements of MAR in relation to the transactions is set out below.

Notes to Editors
Watkin Jones is the UK's leading developer and manager of residential for rent, with a focus on the build to rent, student accommodation and affordable housing sectors. The Group has strong relationships with institutional investors, and a reputation for successful, on-time-delivery of high-quality developments. Since 1999, Watkin Jones has delivered over 51,000 student beds across 150 sites, making it a key player and leader in the UK purpose-built student accommodation market, and is increasingly expanding its operations into the build to rent sector, where it has delivered 3,400 apartments across 19 schemes to date. In addition, Fresh, the Group's specialist accommodation management business, manages c.21,000 student beds and build to rent apartments on behalf of its institutional clients. Watkin Jones has also been responsible for over 80 residential developments, ranging from starter homes to executive housing and apartments.
The Group's competitive advantage lies in its experienced management team and capital-light business model, which enables it to offer an end-to-end solution for investors, delivered entirely in-house with minimal reliance on third parties, across the entire life cycle of an asset.
Watkin Jones was admitted to trading on AIM in March 2016 with the ticker WJG.L. For additional information please visit www.watkinjonesplc.com.
This information is provided by RNS, the news service of the London Stock Exchange. RNS is approved by the Financial Conduct Authority to act as a Primary Information Provider in the United Kingdom. latest.title: Total Voting Rights
latest.content:
1 Jul 2026 08:35
RNS Number : 5834K
Watkin Jones plc
01 July 2026
1 July 2026
Watkin Jones plc
('Watkin Jones' or the 'Company')
Total Voting Rights
In accordance with Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rule 5.6.1, Watkin Jones plc (AIM:WJG) announces that, as at 30 June 2026, its issued share capital consisted of 256,926,493 ordinary shares of £0.01 each. The Company does not hold any shares in treasury.
Therefore, the total number of voting rights in the Company is 256,926,493. This figure may be used by shareholders as the denominator for the calculations by which they may determine if they are required to notify their interest in, or a change to their interest in, the Company under the Financial Conduct Authority's Disclosure and Transparency Rules.
- Ends -

latest.title: Investor Presentation via Investor Meet Company
latest.content:
28 May 2026 15:00
RNS Number : 0814G
Watkin Jones plc
28 May 2026
28 May 2026
Watkin Jones plc
('Watkin Jones' or the 'Group')
Investor Presentation via Investor Meet Company
Watkin Jones is pleased to announce that Alex Pease, Chief Executive Officer, and Simon Jones, Chief Financial Officer, will provide a live presentation relating to its Half Year Results for the half year ended 31 March 2026, via Investor Meet Company on
04 Jun 2026, 13:30 BST
.
The presentation is open to all existing and potential shareholders. Questions can be submitted pre-event via your Investor Meet Company dashboard up until
03 Jun 2026, 09:00 BST
, or at any time during the live presentation.
Investors can sign up to Investor Meet Company for free and add to meet
Watkin Jones
via:
https://www.investormeetcompany.com/watkin-jones-plc/register-investor
.
Investors who already follow Watkin Jones on the Investor Meet Company platform will automatically be invited.
​
- Ends -

company_30s.what_they_do: Watkin Jones plc builds, owns and manages student accommodation and other residential properties across the UK.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The student‑housing market is huge and growing – universities keep expanding and the demand for high‑quality, purpose‑built student homes stays strong.  Watkin Jones owns a large portfolio of these assets, giving it a steady rental income stream and a platform for further development.
company_30s.current_state: The firm is pushing new PBSA projects, dealing with fire‑safety and cladding concerns, and tweaking its corporate structure.  It’s trading at a very low price compared with the value of its assets, suggesting a steep decline from earlier levels.

rns_narrative: The company is tightening governance – new director shareholdings, a change of registered office and updated voting rights.
Its development pipeline is active – a 294‑unit hotel partnership, a joint venture for a £182m PBSA scheme, and a new 294‑unit project with Maslow Capital.
Regulatory worries remain – cladding and fire‑safety issues have prompted a blocklisting application and ongoing scrutiny.

crowd_tape.bull_narrative: Some traders applaud the new PBSA partnership and the hotel project, seeing them as growth catalysts.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Others point to cladding failures and the blocklisting risk, worrying that safety fines could hit the bottom line.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: Early chatter was cautiously hopeful, but the cladding news pushed sentiment into a more negative zone, though the recent partnership has started to lift spirits again.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends is flat – no public buzz, so a surprise catalyst could trigger a large move.

near_term_catalysts: The completion of the 294‑unit hotel partnership could generate a revenue bump.
A positive half‑year earnings report would show the company’s cash flow is stabilising.
Resolution of the cladding/fire‑safety audit – a clean report could lift investor confidence.
A new funding round or share buy‑back would signal management’s belief in the business.
An investor presentation that highlights occupancy above 90% would reinforce the asset quality narrative.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a volume spike of at least twice the average, a clear earnings beat, a clean safety audit, and any announcement that the blocklisting application is withdrawn or resolved.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock sits near the bottom of a deep crash.  APEX shows moderate compression, so the next move will likely come when volume backs a support level and a catalyst confirms that the safety and development issues are resolved.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: The firm has crashed far below its asset value, so a mean‑reversion is likely.  If the price climbs to halfway between the current level and the pre‑crash price, the upside is roughly double the current price.  The deep‑crash signal (likely green or yellow) historically delivers large rebounds, and the new PBSA projects add a clear upside driver.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: Regulatory fines from cladding failures could erode earnings.  If the safety audit fails or the blocklisting stays in place, the price may stay low.  A continued lack of revenue growth could keep the stock stuck in a low‑price trap.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential loss: the price could fall a further 10% of the pre‑crash level.  Potential gain: a 200% rise to halfway pre‑crash.  The upside is at least twice the risk, making the trade asymmetrical.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter when price touches the key support level and volume confirms a bounce.  Size the position to 5% of your portfolio, and place a stop 30% below support to protect against a further dip.
playbook.if_watching: Hold cash until you see a volume surge, a positive earnings beat, or a clean safety audit.  If any of those appear, consider a small entry to test the rebound.
playbook.if_passing: None identified – no imminent delisting or bankruptcy risk.

ai_insights.bottom_line: Watkin Jones sits at a deep‑crashed bottom; if safety issues are cleared and the new PBSA projects deliver, the price could rebound to mid‑pre‑crash levels, offering a high upside while the risk of a further drop is limited.

Historical data shows 4 rallies averaging 30% upside. Current position: -34.9%. Your edge comes from waiting for AI Engine confirmation instead of blind entry.
